finding: Across 13 extended stress scenarios, the proposed STHG-CMAPPO risk-service policy improved mean reward by 35.35% on average over the strongest risk-avoidance baseline.
The proposed policy achieved average risk reduction of 8.61% and average delay reduction of 17.03% under extended stress testing.
Average service improvement was 15.25% and average resilience improvement was 9.82% across stress scenarios.
The strongest reward gain was observed under supplier_shock_severe with 45.78% reward improvement.
The lowest reward gain was observed under normal with 26.63% reward improvement.
The proposed policy passed 100.00% of stress scenarios using the five-metric robustness scorecard.
